title: Oxanthera neocaledonica
intro: Oxanthera neocaledonica, also known as New Caledonian Oxanthera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It has been traditionally used in Pacific Island medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its reported health benefits of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include oxantherin and other alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Oxanthera neocaledonica is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
